data.title: Microservices Architecture
data.description: Google employs a microservices architecture allowing services to be independently developed and deployed.
data.technical_spec: Uses containerization with Kubernetes for managing microservices.

data.title: Load Balancing
data.description: Advanced load balancing techniques ensure even distribution of traffic across servers.
data.technical_spec: Uses proprietary global load balancing system with DNS-based and direct routing methods.

data.title: Scalable Data Infrastructure
data.description: Google’s architecture scales horizontally, allowing it to handle massive data volumes.
data.technical_spec: Incorporates NoSQL databases like Bigtable combined with distributed file storage systems.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: Leverages a global CDN to accelerate content delivery and reduce latency.
data.technical_spec: Operates Edge Points for caching content close to users.

data.title: Fault Tolerance
data.description: Ensures system resilience and uptime through redundancy and failovers.
data.technical_spec: Implements failover strategies using distributed systems and replication.
